title: Missing rank argument that top-sigma barrier implies all eigenvalues below u_sigma

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Step 1 sets sigma = floor(eps*n/42) and concludes: 'once we reach |S| = sigma+1 with B^{u_sigma}_sigma(S) finite, every eigenvalue of tL_S lies below eps.' The inference relies on the fact that ALL eigenvalues of tL_S (not just the top sigma of them) are below u_sigma, but this is only stated, not proved.

The argument requires the observation: since G_S is a graph on |S| = sigma+1 vertices, rank(L_S) <= |S| - 1 = sigma, so rank(tL_S) <= sigma. Hence tL_S has at most sigma nonzero eigenvalues, and tracking the TOP sigma eigenvalues via B^u_sigma covers ALL nonzero eigenvalues of tL_S. Thus B^{u_sigma}_sigma(tL_S) < infinity implies each of the (at most sigma) nonzero eigenvalues of tL_S is strictly below u_sigma <= eps, giving ||tL_S||_op <= eps and hence eps*L >= L_S.

This rank counting is the KEY INNOVATION making the bounded-rank barrier work instead of a full barrier. The proof mentions it in the Remarks section ('we track only the top sigma') but never formally verifies it in the proof body. Without this, the conclusion that the operator norm condition (1) is satisfied is unjustified.

comments.author: system
comments.body:
**Proof file:** `webapp/issues/first_proof_1/q6/working_solution.tex`

The issue described above occurs in this working solution file. Reviewers should consult the file at that path for the exact context.
